title: The Reverie Opens Under Hidden Shadows
description: On pages 2–5, this part focuses on Unknown person #1, Marines, Unknown person #2, Kiku, Garp, Unknown person #3 as The Reverie gathers world leaders while hidden powers at Mariejois begin to move.
scene: World leaders gather at Mariejois while the Revolutionary Army and the secret ruler behind the Empty Throne move in the shadows.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Whole cake island

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Far across the seas...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: ZzTT...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Are you kidding?! I refuse!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Look, it doesn't really have anything to do with you.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It's just that if I'm not the one to take straw hat's head, I'll be shamed forever!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He has something he needs to return to me!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Besides,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You still owe me one big time, Kaido.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: That's all in the past!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Nope. That debt is life- long.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'm the one with a bone to pick with that kid!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: If you come here, I'm killing you, Linlin!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Do your worst, then. I just figured I'd drop you a line.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Listen up. I'm going to be the one to kill straw hat.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Let's stay friendly on this, eh?! like good old times!

dialogue.speaker: Marines
dialogue.text: Marine HQ

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Big Mom's on the move!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: She's going to make con- tact with Kaido!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Their signal is completely unenscripted.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It's almost too easy for us to tap into!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Which means they're probably saying, "Come and stop us if you can," right?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Stay friendly...this

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The might we have that could possibly stop this are... the admirals or the shichibukai. That's it.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Shall I go, then, Sakazuki?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Hold, borsalino.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The strength of the armies of the Wano Country are as yet unknown.

dialogue.speaker: Kiku
dialogue.text: The Samurai, eh...

dialogue.speaker: Garp
dialogue.text: Are you serious, Garp?!

dialogue.speaker: Garp
dialogue.text: They're after your grandson! Straw hat luffy!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: PPPPFFETIT! Hilarious!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: It's no laughing matter!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Do you really want to just let two yonkou meet each other like this?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: "Let"? You don't get it.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It's reverie right now. The people that could stop this meeting are all here, as Royal bodyguards!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Sorry to butt in, lady, but about big mom...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: She's definitely taken all that into account! What did boss Sakazuki say?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: "Because the Wano country is not part of the WG alliance, they are outside our jurisdiction." Ugh!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: I knew it!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Buwa- Haha!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Not messing with them right now is our best bet!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But, GARP... All this talk of big mom and Kaido...

summary: This section advances the Reverie storyline with 42 dialogue beats. The Reverie looks ceremonial on the surface, but Imu, the Five Elders, and the Revolutionaries turn it into a major political pivot.
